paper_title: Structural study of biologically significant ligands with major birch pollen allergen Betv1 by docking and molecular dynamics simulation

methods.description: This method describes the docking procedure used to study the interaction between biologically significant ligands and the major birch pollen allergen Betv1. The process involves preparing the protein and ligand structures, setting up the docking environment, and performing docking simulations to predict the binding orientations and energies.

steps.description: The crystal structure of Betv1 (pdb code: 1BV1) was obtained from PDB.
steps.searchable_summary: download Betv1 crystal structure from PDB

steps.description: The structures of ANS, kinetin, flavone and naringenin were downloaded from Cambridge Structural Database (CSD).
inputs: ligand names: ANS, kinetin, flavone, naringenin

steps.description: All other ligands were constructed using the Biopolymer utility of the software Insight II.
steps.searchable_summary: construct ligand structures using Insight II

steps.description: Docking calculations were carried out using AutoDock 3.0.5.
The grid maps representing the protein in the actual docking process were calculated with the aid of AutoGrid.

The dimension of the grid was i) 40X40X40 points in each dimension for smaller ligands (ANS and lower chain length fatty acids) and ii) 60X60X60 points in each dimension for rest with spacing of 0.375 Å between the grid points.
steps.searchable_summary: set grid dimensions and spacing for docking

steps.description: Gasteiger charges computed by ADT (AutoDock tools) were used on the atoms for each ligand.
The AUTOTORS utility, included in the AutoDock software, was used to define all possible torsions of ligand molecules for the docking algorithm.

Docking simulations were carried out using the Lamarckian Genetic Algorithm, with an initial population of 150 randomly placed individuals, a maximum number of 2,50,000 energy evaluations, a mutation rate of 0.02, a cross over rate of 0.08 and an elitism value of 1.
steps.searchable_summary: perform docking simulations with Lamarckian Genetic Algorithm

steps.description: For the local search, the pseudo-Solis and Wets algorithm was applied.
steps.searchable_summary: apply pseudo-Solis and Wets algorithm for local search

steps.description: Each job consisted of 100 independent runs.

Finally, resulting docking orientations lying within 1.5 Ǻ in the root-mean square deviation (rmsd) tolerance of each other were clustered together and represented by the result with the most favorable free energy of binding (∆G b ).
steps.searchable_summary: cluster docking results by rmsd and select by free energy
